===================
=== Dylan1981-06-30.txt
===================
===================
===================
Bob Dylan
Earls Court
London, England
30 June 1981
  
Unknown Audience>CDR>EAC>FLAC Level 8

 

 
  
Gotta Serve Somebody
I Believe In You
Like A Rolling Stone
Till I Get It Right (Red Lane/Larry Henley)
Man Gave Names To All The Animals
Maggie's Farm
Simple Twist Of Fate
Ballad Of A Thin Man
Girl From The North Country
Dead Man, Dead Man
Forever Young
Abraham, Martin And John (Dick Holler)
Slow Train
  

Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues
In The Summertime
Mr. Tambourine Man
Solid Rock
Just Like A Woman
Watered-Down Love
What Can I Do For You
All Along The Watchtower
Lenny Bruce
When You Gonna Wake Up
In The Garden
Blowin' In The Wind
Don't Think Twice, It's All Right
Knockin' On Heaven's Door
  

 

 

Concert # 7 of The Europe Summer Tour 1981. 1981 concert #11.

Bob Dylan (vocal & guitar), Fred Tackett (guitar), Steve Ripley (guitar), Willie Smith (keyboards), Tim Drummond (bass), Jim Keltner (drums), Clydie King, Carolyn Dennis, Regina Havis, Madelyn Quebec (background vocals).

 

4 Regina Havis (vocal).

12 Bob Dylan (piano & vocal), Clydie King (shared vocal).

26 Bob Dylan solo (vocal & guitar).



BobTalk

Thank you. This is another animal song I wrote, a long time ago. It's about the man animal. (before Maggie's Farm)

Thank you. This is a new song we recently recorded, called Dead Man, Dead Man When Will You Arise.

Thank you. I wanna introduce the man on the keyboards tonight, from Portsmouth, Virginia, give him a big hand, Willie Smith. Standing next to him, on the guitar, Fred Tackett. Standing next to him, on the other guitar, Steve Ripley. On the left over there, the girl you met earlier, Carolyn Dennis. Standing next to her. Madelyn Quebec. Right, Regina McCreary, Clydie King. Standing next to her, Tim Drummond. Well, I hope you heard something that you came to hear. (....) Hard to judge how we're doing by these monitors on stage. Hard to tell what is coming across out there. Anyway, on the drums, a man who's truly a legend in his own lifetime. Not only his lifetime, but plenty other peoples lifetime. On the drums, ladies and gentlemen. from Tulsa, Oklahoma, Jimmy Lee Keltner. Ha-ha. I'd like to introduce all you people to, but I don't know your names. All right. Thank you. (at the end of In The Garden)

 

Bootlegs

Footsteps 81. LP

Gospel Rock And More. White Wonder LP.

 

5 new songs (18%) compared to previous concert. 1 new song for this tour.

 

Stereo audience recording, 155 minutes.

Audience video recording, 115 minutes.



All info complied from:  http://www.bjorner.com/DSN06290%20-%201981%20Europe%20Summer%20Tour.htm#DSN06350
